title: Erdős' question on c_2
statement_text: What is the largest real number $c_2$ such that every two-coloring of the positive integers has a color class for which the upper logarithmic density of its set of subset sums is at least $c_2$? More generally, what is $c_r := \min_{\mathbb{N}=A_1\sqcup\cdots\sqcup A_r} \max_{i\in[r]} \bar{d}_{\ell}(\Sigma(A_i))$?
context_text: Erdős introduced $c_r$ in his problem papers and noted that every $r$-coloring has some color class whose subset sums have upper logarithmic density at least $1/2$, and upper density $1$. He observed that an explicit two-coloring (where $n$ receives color based on $\lfloor\log_4\log_2 n\rfloor \bmod 2$) shows each color class's subset sums has upper logarithmic density $14/15 < 1$, motivating the question of the exact value of $c_2$.
paper_contribution: Proves that $c_2 = (2+\sqrt{3})/4 \approx 0.93301$, fully answering Erdős' forty-year-old question; also establishes the upper bound $c_r \leq \left(1-\frac{1}{2b_0}\right)\left(1+\frac{1}{2^r b_0 - r}\right)$ for all $r\geq 2$, where $b_0>1$ is the unique root of $b^r-2rb+r-1=0$.
notes: Attributed year unknown (bibliography not in extracted text); described as 'forty-year-old' in a paper posted May 2021, suggesting circa 1981. References cited as [3, 4] in the paper.
paper_title: The upper logarithmic density of monochromatic subset sums

title: Conjecture on tightness of the upper bound for all r ≥ 3
statement_text: We suspect that our upper bound $c_r \leq \left(1-\frac{1}{2b_0}\right)\left(1+\frac{1}{2^r b_0 - r}\right)$ is also tight for all $r \geq 3$, where $b_0 > 1$ is the unique root of $b^r - 2rb + r - 1 = 0$.
context_text: Theorem 1 gives a general upper bound for $c_r$ for all $r\geq 2$ and proves it is achieved (i.e., tight) for $r=2$, yielding $c_2=(2+\sqrt{3})/4$. The authors believe the same formula gives the exact value for all larger $r$, but remark that their methods are insufficient to prove this, directing the reader to brief concluding remarks for further discussion.
notes: Stated in prose without a labelled environment: 'We suspect that our upper bound is also tight for all r ≥ 3, but our methods do not seem sufficient for proving this.' PDF extraction may have garbled exponent notation (2^r vs 2r) in the formula.
paper_title: The upper logarithmic density of monochromatic subset sums
